★ ISSUE 08 · E-NAME CARD × NREA 2026

Tomorrow at NREA — one QR, every contact tracked.

Skip the paper namecard. Share your E-Name Card QR instead — see exactly who saved you.

Tomorrow, NREA 2026 puts 200+ of PropNex Malaysia’s people in one ballroom. That’s 200 handshakes, 200 exchanges, 200 chances for someone to save your contact — or lose it. This week, we’re showing you how the E-Name Card turns tomorrow night into a tracked lead list. Create your NREA 2026 link tonight, share the QR at your table, and check your Dashboard for the scans. Plus this week’s Insider Edge shows you 3 moves to turn NREA into a whole year of content, and Binastra Cochrane unlocks 4% commission on the first 30 units.

A Note to Agents

Five minutes here. Pick what helps. Run it this week.

★ INSIDER EDGE · ISSUE 08

Tomorrow you walk into NREA 2026. Here’s how to walk out with a whole year of content.

Every year, agents show up to the ceremony, take some photos, and let them sit in the gallery. This week — 3 moves to turn tomorrow night into the heaviest piece of content in your year. Whether you’re on stage or watching from your table.

PropNex Malaysia
The Insider Edge

明天就是 NREA 2026。National Real Estate Awards,是全馬房地產這一年最高的一場典禮,PropNex Malaysia 這一晚也將見證我們自己的 moment。

但每一年,我們都看到同一件事:agent 到場了,穿了正裝、拍了照,然後那些照片就一直留在手機裡。

這一期,我們想在你走進宴會廳之前,先說清楚三件事:明晚,你的鏡頭對準什麼、文字怎麼寫、Story 多久發一次。不管你今晚是上台領獎的那一個,還是在座位上見證的那一個,這三件事都一樣用得上。

1. 鏡頭對準什麼

明晚鏡頭對準什麼,決定你能說什麼故事

NREA 的素材不是越多越好,而是要對準幾個真正有內容力的時刻。

第一類是象徵型素材:舞台上的 NREA 2026 獎座、鎂光燈打下來的頒獎那一秒、獎座近距離的細節。這些畫面不用你多說,本身就代表「我在一個 industry 最頂級的場合」。

第二類是規模型素材:整個宴會廳幾百位 agent 一起盛裝出席、PropNex 團隊拍全體大合照、老闆親自出席。這些畫面傳遞的是「我在一間認真做事的公司」,鏡頭帶到就夠了。

第三類是關係型素材:你和同事、和 leader、和台上領獎人的合照。這類讓你的 Story 有溫度,也讓觀眾看到你是誰的圈子裡的人。

進宴會廳之前先想好你要抓的三個時刻,素材就不會只是留在相簿裡。

2. 文字決定你是打卡還是品牌

同一張獎座照,文字角度決定你是打卡還是做品牌

拍到素材是第一步,文字才是讓 Story 真正發揮作用的地方。

行銷上有一個概念叫 Value Framing:同一件事,不同角度寫,在別人心裡建立的形象完全不同。同樣一場 NREA,你可以寫「出席 NREA 2026」,也可以寫「今晚看到 [同事名] 三年前還在別家,現在上台領這座獎」。前者是打卡,後者是在說你認同什麼、你身邊有誰。

就算你今晚沒有到場,這個邏輯一樣適用。看到公司放出 NREA 的動態、看到某個名字上台,你用你自己的角度回應,說說「這讓我想到」或「我選擇留在 PropNex 的原因之一,就是這個」,一樣是在做個人品牌。你說的是 event,傳遞的是你。

同一張獎座照,兩個方向:一個讓人知道你在場,一個讓人想認識你這個人。

3. Story 的頻率就是存在感

Story 的頻率,就是你明晚在 IG 的存在感

很多人怕發太多 Story 會打擾人,但 Instagram 的邏輯剛好相反。

Story 是按時間排的,你最近發的一篇就排在關注者 Story 欄的最前面。明晚你每發一篇 Story,就等於重新出現在他們眼前一次。整晚下來,我們建議至少 5 篇,讓你的名字在這一晚一直出現在最顯眼的位置:走紅毯的時候一篇、進場座位一篇、頒獎那一刻一篇、跟同事合照一篇、結束合影一篇。

Story 也是你跟關注者關係最直接的一條線。當有人為你的 Story 傳 DM 給你,Instagram 把這個記為一個強訊號,之後會把你的內容推給他更多。這就像你在一個場合裡跟某個人聊起來,對方之後就開始記得你,Instagram 也是一樣的邏輯。

明晚至少 5 篇。不用每篇都精彩,讓人看到「你今晚在 NREA」,就已經在建立存在感了。

Story 不是為了說一件大事,是為了讓人習慣看到你。

明晚 NREA 2026 是一個很好的起點,但這樣的大場合一年只有幾次。真正持續做品牌的人,不會只等大典禮才動,因為讓別人相信你的東西,從來不只在頒獎現場。

公司每天撐在你後面的那個平台、你早就習以為常的資源,才是你長期的底氣。明晚只是把這一切放大到最亮的一晚。不管你今晚有沒有上台,都能把這一晚變成你這一整年最有 weight 的內容。

下一期,我們接著說:NREA 之後那一整個禮拜,怎麼把這一晚的素材拉長成你整個月的存在感。

Tomorrow is NREA 2026. The National Real Estate Awards, the biggest ceremony in Malaysia's property industry this year, and the night PropNex Malaysia gets its own moment.

And every year, we see the same thing. Agents show up, put on the suit, take some photos, and those photos never leave the gallery.

Before you walk into the ballroom tomorrow, three things worth knowing: what to shoot, how to write, and how often to post. Whether you're the one walking up to the stage or the one watching from your table, these three moves work the same way.

1. Know What to Shoot

Know what to shoot before the lights go down

It's not about shooting everything. It's about knowing which moments are actually worth capturing.

The first type: symbolism moments. The NREA 2026 trophy on stage. The spotlight moment when the award is handed over. A close-up of the trophy itself. These shots don't need explaining, they say "I was at the peak of the industry."

The second type: scale moments. A ballroom filled with hundreds of agents in gowns and suits. The full PropNex team group photo. Your boss flying in for the night. "I'm at a company that means business" — the shot does the work.

The third type: connection moments. A photo with your colleague, with your leader, or with the person who just walked off stage. These make your Stories feel real, and they let people see whose circle you're standing in.

Decide on three moments you want to capture before you walk into the ballroom. That's what stops the photos from living in your gallery forever.

2. Caption Makes the Brand

Same trophy shot. Different caption. Completely different brand.

Capturing the shot is step one. But your words are what make the Story actually land.

There's a marketing concept called Value Framing: the same moment, told from a different angle, builds a completely different impression. You can post "Attended NREA 2026" — or you can post "The teammate who was at another agency three years ago is on stage tonight." One is a check-in. The other tells people what you believe and who you're standing with.

This works even if you're not in the room. When the company posts NREA moments, when a name gets called — respond with your own angle. "This is exactly why I chose to stay at PropNex." You're talking about the event. But people are seeing you.

Same trophy photo, two directions: one tells people you were there, the other makes them want to know who you are.

3. Post Often, Stay Visible

The more you post tomorrow, the more you show up

A lot of people hold back because they think posting too many Stories feels like spamming. Instagram actually thinks the opposite.

Stories are ranked by recency, the most recent one sits at the front of your followers' Story tray. Every time you post during NREA, you move back to the front. Five Stories minimum across the night: the red carpet arrival, entering your seat, the award moment, a photo with your colleagues, the closing group shot.

Stories are also the most direct line between you and the people who follow you. When someone DMs you because of a Story, Instagram treats that as a strong signal and pushes your content to that person more. Think of it like actually starting a conversation at an event, people remember the ones who talked to them.

Five Stories minimum. They don't all need to be perfect. Let people see that you were at NREA tonight — that presence alone is how you build visibility.

Stories aren't about saying one big thing. They're about making it a habit for people to see you.

Tomorrow night is a great place to start. But a ceremony this size only rolls around once a year. The people who consistently build a brand don't wait for the big stage, because what makes others trust you was never just the award night.

The platform quietly behind you every day, the resources you've stopped noticing, those are what you actually stand on long-term. Tomorrow just turns the volume up. Whether you're on stage or not, you can turn tomorrow night into the heaviest piece of content in your entire year.

Next issue: after NREA. How to stretch tomorrow night's material into a whole month of visibility. See you next week.

Why it matters tomorrow Tomorrow at your table, share the QR instead of a paper namecard. Every person who scans gets tracked to that specific link. So you don’t just know how many saved you — you know they came from NREA.
